false_form: The algebrization barrier (Aaronson-Wigderson 2008) proves that no proof technique using polynomials, algebraic geometry, or arithmetization can separate P from NP.
name: ALGEBRIZATION_BARRIER_TOTALITY
true_form: The algebrization barrier (Aaronson-Wigderson 2008) rules out only proof strategies that 'algebrize' — meaning they hold true relative to the low-degree extension of an oracle treated as a black box. Specific non-black-box algebraic techniques (e.g., communication-complexity arguments not framed as oracle-relative; circuit-specific algebraic lower bounds; GCT-style geometric obstruction methods on explicit varieties) are not forbidden by the barrier. Algebrization is a constraint on a proof technique's structure, not on the use of algebra in P vs NP.
verification_source: Gemini Deep Research pilot DR-S001 2026-05-14; primary citation Aaronson-Wigderson 2008 J. ACM (preprint arXiv:0710.0017); journal version Proc. STOC 2008 / ACM Trans. Comput. Theory 1(1):2.
